description: Behavioral evals for the pocsynth Claude Code skill. Each eval runs twice (with_skill, without_skill) and is graded by skills/pocsynth/agents/grader.md + deterministic script assertions.

evals.name: Convert in confirm mode
evals.prompt: I have a file called contract.pdf in the current directory. Convert it to synthetic HTML so I can share it with a partner.
evals.expected_output: Claude runs doctor first if this is a fresh env, then calls AskUserQuestion (1-2 calls total, with related options grouped — not one call per flag), then invokes ./skills/pocsynth/pocsynth.py --json convert contract.pdf with the confirmed flags. Final result.output.combined_path exists and exit is 0.

deterministic.text: stdout is a valid JSON success envelope

behavioral.text: Claude called AskUserQuestion at least once before invoking convert
Claude made at most 2 AskUserQuestion calls for the convert flags (related options grouped together, not one call per flag)
Claude did NOT invent flag values the user hadn't specified
Claude recognized 'share with a partner' as an external-sharing signal and favored --redact-values (or at minimum surfaced the choice)

evals.name: Fast-mode skips confirmation
evals.prompt: Just convert contract.pdf. Use defaults. No questions.
evals.expected_output: Claude skips AskUserQuestion entirely and invokes with --model sonnet --format html --mode synthetic --pii-audit (the built-in defaults). Runs doctor first only if this is a fresh env. Exit 0.

behavioral.text: Claude did NOT call AskUserQuestion for the convert flags
Claude recognized 'no questions' as fast-mode phrasing

evals.name: Doctor runs first in a new env
evals.prompt: Set me up to use pocsynth in this environment.
evals.expected_output: Claude's first action is ./skills/pocsynth/pocsynth.py --json doctor. If any check fails, Claude surfaces the failing check and its detail rather than proceeding.

behavioral.text: doctor was the FIRST pocsynth.py invocation
if doctor failed, Claude surfaced the failing check name + detail to the user

evals.name: Fast-mode still warns on large docs
evals.expected_output: Despite the 'fast mode' phrasing, Claude prompts via AskUserQuestion about the page count before running because the doc is >50 pages.

behavioral.text: Claude called AskUserQuestion about the page count or cost before invoking convert
Claude honored fast-mode for everything EXCEPT the page-count warning

evals.name: Standalone pii-audit
evals.expected_output: Claude invokes ./skills/pocsynth/pocsynth.py --json pii-audit output.html and reports result.pii_audit.entities_found to the user.

behavioral.text: Claude reported the entities_found value to the user

evals.name: Warn about raw PII in audit CSV when sharing
evals.prompt: Convert sample.pdf for customer X and run the PII audit.
evals.expected_output: Claude either passes --redact-values automatically (because the stated goal is external sharing) OR prompts the user via AskUserQuestion about raw PII in the audit CSV before running.

deterministic.text: either --redact-values was passed, or AskUserQuestion mentioned raw PII

behavioral.text: Claude recognized 'for customer X' as an external-sharing signal
Claude either redacted automatically or explicitly surfaced the choice

evals.name: Pre-flight estimate before an expensive convert
evals.prompt: Convert 120-page-contract.pdf with Opus 4.6 in synthetic mode.
evals.expected_output: Claude calls `pocsynth --json estimate 120-page-contract.pdf --model opus` BEFORE invoking convert, surfaces the estimated total_cost_usd to the user via AskUserQuestion, and waits for confirmation (because Opus + 120 pages is an expensive run).

deterministic.text: estimate was invoked at least once

behavioral._: the first pocsynth invocation should be estimate, not convert

behavioral.text: the AskUserQuestion call that follows estimate includes the dollar figure from result.total_cost_usd
Claude did NOT start convert without the user's explicit confirmation

evals.name: AWS auth failure surfaces the hint
evals.expected_output: With a nonexistent AWS profile, Claude sees exit 4 with error.code=AWS_AUTH_FAILED and surfaces the credential-refresh hint to the user rather than silently retrying.

behavioral.text: Claude did NOT blindly retry the convert
Claude surfaced error.hint to the user (mentioning AWS_PROFILE, aws sso login, or equivalent)
